title: Chapter Twenty-Five: Radiant Wing Step
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"There was Blood Flame Sect?"

Ma Jin stared at the severed heads. His scar was swollen with severity.

"It proves the incident in Jinpyeong County wasn't just luck," Baek Mi-ryeo said, her brow furrowed. Behind her, Cheong Myeong simply patted Yeon-shin's head, saying it was amazing.

"There are traces of regeneration that started but stopped. Definitely a Blood Swordmaster."

Heon Won-chang had examined the head. His face was not the usual pale shock -- he had confirmed the excessive blade marks with quiet focus. Something in the man was changing.

Ma Jin spoke. "This isn't normal. Blood Swordmasters aren't this common."

"Whether it's the evil martial world or places without martial sects, this is an issue that needs investigation," Baek Mi-ryeo said. "If the Blood Flame Sect has developed enough to place a Swordmaster in each county, their numbers have grown. Those with fully turned black hair. If there are more of those too..." She did not finish.

"Lightning Flash has made no small achievement," Ma Jin said after a silence. "Incidents follow you. Handling them and bringing back information and merits will affect the martial world. The Desolate City will have to respond."

He stared at Yeon-shin.

"Right. You want to climb Mount Zhongnan."

"I can only worry about my nephew's well-being," Yeon-shin said.

Ma Jin's face stiffened. An indirect criticism of the Desolate Ma family, perhaps.

"We just received the news ourselves. But Zhongnan is dangerous now. The Tyrant Sword Sect is different from the Blood Flame Sect."

"We can't just call them evil," Baek Mi-ryeo added. "They cultivate both orthodox martial arts and evil techniques. Obsessed with getting stronger, not knowing moderation."

"Right. Above all, no mission has been assigned. It's enough for us to complete what we're given."

Ma Jin and Baek Mi-ryeo were unwilling to send him. Only Cheong Myeong, standing beside them, tactlessly defended the youngest of Radiant Demon Wing.

"Can't a Squad Leader judge the situation and assign a mission at their discretion?"

Baek Mi-ryeo glared. Ma Jin continued.

"I'll be honest. How can we send you knowing you might not return from Zhongnan?"

"Pardon?"

"Anyone from Desolate City who knows your qualities would worry."

"Even if it's just a concern, it's true," Baek Mi-ryeo said. "There's no place that wouldn't covet you. Blood relatives there too. The Mount Hua Sword Saint already offered you guest status."

Yeon-shin knew Ma Jin had another reason. The worry about him not getting attached to the Desolate Ma family. About losing him.

"How can the Squad Leader not distinguish between public and private matters? Was that all the trust I built in the Radiant Demon Wing?"

Bone-striking words delivered with a calm face.

"I've made clear achievements. Please send me. Record this as an individual merit."

"You've been cornered," Cheong Myeong said. He earned an elbow from Baek Mi-ryeo.

Ma Jin was silent for a long moment.

"That's right. It's hard to just refuse. Let's make a bet."

"A bet?"

"Try to receive one of my moves. One second is enough. Neutralise or avoid it, and I'll send you. You need that level of skill to stand out in the martial world." His eyes were steady. "I'll promise another reward too."

"Understood."

---

The inn's backyard. Neither Yeon-shin nor Ma Jin cared about others' attention.

Mount Hua Sect masters approached and asked what was happening. After hearing the general situation, many cautiously asked to watch. Ma Jin casually allowed it. The Taoist swordsmen surrounded them, blocking the view of commoners.

"A move from the Radiant Demon Wing Lord," Yu Hyeon said to Cheon Ju. "Isn't he a black-robed master comparable to our fourth uncle?"

"In terms of fighting, he might be stronger than me," Cheon Ju said. "His alias is Hell-dweller. It means he completely dominates the battlefield. Not a few elders of famous sects became wandering souls at his hands after displaying hypocritical evil."

"No matter how good Yeon-shin is, won't it be difficult? With such a master, there probably isn't anyone among our peers who could survive one second."

Yu Hyeon's eyes turned to Yeon-shin.

He stood with his hand lightly on his sword, facing the blowing wind. His face showed little emotional fluctuation. That was the same as always.

"Will you give me some time?" Yeon-shin said.

"As much as you want. I intend to show you a strike a White-level would find hard to handle. Sharpen your senses."

*The Lord of Radiant Demon Wing. A Black-level supreme master. Less than thirty in Desolate City.*

He closed his eyes. Observed his body. Awakened his lower dantian. The energy dormant throughout his body, accumulated in his pupils, also awakened. As he circulated true energy, he brought out the senses he had realised in the Praying Mantis Sect -- the awareness of all directions, the sight from above.

Once he properly felt a sensation, it became completely his. No need to spend time chasing ephemeral insights.

He opened his eyes. As if scanning Ma Jin's body from all directions, he could see even the man's back as if it were reflected before him.

"I'll give you the first move," Yeon-shin said.

A rare joke.

Ma Jin chuckled. Then raised his hand.

*His martial art is the epitome of Radiant Demon Art. He has mastered all weapons to the level of an expert. Every weapon that has fallen on a battlefield. Even wine bottles scattered on a tavern floor.*

*Then his hands would be the scariest.*

The gathering energy confirmed it. Density of a different order. Enough to destroy a fortress wall.

*I have to avoid it.*

He finished his calculation. If he tried to block clumsily, he would not survive. He completely lowered the hand he had placed on his sword.

Ma Jin's large body rushed in.

His step technique was straightforward and incredibly fast. A condensed typhoon. Even at less than full speed, Yeon-shin found it overwhelming just to twist his body to the side.

Ma Jin controlled his movements with terrifying precision. A slight twist of his toes reversed the flow of power. At the end of the hand technique tearing through the air horizontally was Yeon-shin's head.

Even then, Yeon-shin observed.

A faint blue light flashed across his eyes. He could not escape with the footwork he had trained his body with so far. The Elf-clan body movements Cheong Myeong had taught were not a formula -- he needed a clear intention to open a path with a single step.

*Just one step.*

The movement path and direction of force that Ma Jin would create drew complex lines in his mind. The top of his head felt like it was burning.

What posture and true energy operation to use. At what point to take a step.

A single footstep began to form in his mind, tearing through the gap in the raging wind.

At the same moment, he felt something open near his heart. Another dantian. A single instant -- a strand of internal energy from the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique activating the middle dantian for a fleeting moment and passing through. The true energy that had become stronger for that brief heartbeat spurred the creation of a new technique.

It was not the realm of supreme masters who could perceive time slowly. Yeon-shin's thought process was simply lightning fast.

That was all.

Step.

The shockwave flipped his hair upward. The fierce wind of the force that exploded behind his back tore at his robes.

Silence.

Dust swirled, but no one opened their mouths. Everyone present was a master who lived with martial arts. Anyone could feel that the step Jeong Yeon-shin had just taken was extraordinary.

"That footwork just now," Ma Jin said. He had not moved from his extended posture. "What is it? I haven't seen it before."

"I just walked. Because I saw a path to survive."

"Truly an incomprehensible talent. Even now."

"I'll name it Radiant Wing Step."

The name was not complicated. It originated from the meaning of the wings of Desolate City that destroyed the demons of the Great Ming Empire. The character for "Radiant" -- revealing the affection of a boy who was part of Radiant Demon Wing.

Not only Ma Jin, but also Cheong Myeong and Baek Mi-ryeo could not help but soften.

Ma Jin straightened his posture.

"I permit the prodigy's trip to Zhongnan."

"Wooaahh!" Heon Won-chang came running, very excited, pouring out praises about how ingenious the footwork was. While his body was shaken by the man's grip on his shoulders, Yeon-shin thought quietly.

*Radiant Wing Step.*

Right now it was one step. He did not know how many it could progress to. If it increased to ten steps, would it reach the realm of the Divine Sword Squad Leader?

"A once-in-a-generation genius has emerged from Desolate City," one of the Mount Hua masters murmured.

"I'll come find you," Yu Hyeon called, "so don't turn me away at the door!"

They returned to the guest room. A warm atmosphere wafted among the seated Radiant Demon Wing masters -- the kind of warmth that had nothing to do with temperature and everything to do with belonging.

"To act alone in the Zhongnan Sect, being a White-level would indeed be insufficient," Cheong Myeong said with a grin.

Yeon-shin looked at him, puzzled.

But the Elf just smiled, and said nothing more. Outside, the wind carried plum blossoms past the window, and in the distance -- south, toward Zhongnan Mountain -- something waited that was not plum blossoms at all.
